_comment: Single source of truth for the NSEdge cross-simulator benchmark. All engine runners read these parameters so the workload is identical. Operating point is the CPU-validated regime of the NSEdge paper (measured Intel-i5 matmul profile, ~5 Hz per edge worker); GPU/high-fps DipDCE is deferred (no measured GPU profile).

workload.name: DipDCE edge-offload (object-detection inference frames)
workload.compute_model: measured Intel-i5 matmul profile (~132 ms per 180 KB frame); injected identically across engines

network.note: cloud-offload fraction is a saturation-free high-latency sink modelled identically across engines; the packet-level comparison targets the edge path

scalability_sweep.note: aggregate load scales as 5 x workers; wall-clock vs workers is the scalability metric

physical_ground_truth.source: NSEdge paper validation: tc-netem shaped loopback, 4 workers, 5 Hz, 180 KB

measurement.speed_metric: wall-clock seconds to simulate sim_duration_s, single run, /usr/bin/time -v (peak RSS)
